assertions.title: Architecture enriched with Rust and BRAID consensus

steps.action: Open #graph-architecture in the real graph shell
steps.expected: Architecture surface shows the new execution-moat enrichment block
steps.observed: Focused Architecture surface rendered an 'Execution moat additions' section with the new enrichment cards.

steps.expected: Rust stack details and BRAID 4-way consensus show 88% and 100% win-rate figures
steps.observed: Visible copy included Rust execution lane details plus BRAID consensus bullets with '88% win rate' and '100% win rate'.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed; repeated agent-browser errors checks returned no entries while validating Architecture.
evidence.network: No API/XHR behavior was required for this surface; agent-browser network log returned no captured requests.
sourceInspection: src/components/graph/surfaces/ArchitectureSurface.tsx:21-48 renders the architecture enrichment block with BetterCard shells.
src/content/enriched-surfaces.ts:16-34 defines the Rust execution lane and BRAID 4-way consensus bullets, including 88% and 100% win rates.

steps.expected: Tokenomics surface shows new flywheel revenue additions
steps.observed: Focused Tokenomics surface rendered a 'Flywheel revenue additions' section above the broader tokenomics content.

steps.expected: Surface explains arbitrage flywheel revenue and buy/sell tax routing
steps.observed: Visible copy described vBETTER/TRUTH-PERP arbitrage revenue plus a live '2% buy + 2% sell' treasury tax route.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed; repeated agent-browser errors checks returned no entries while validating Tokenomics.
evidence.network: No API/XHR behavior was required for this surface; agent-browser network log returned no captured requests.
sourceInspection: src/content/enriched-surfaces.ts:39-67 defines the arbitrage-flywheel revenue block and the live buy/sell tax routing block.

assertions.title: New nodes have graph edges to existing topology

steps.expected: The default state shows all four new nodes as part of the atlas topology
steps.observed: The default overview exposed clickable graph buttons for Macro Thesis, HFT Edge, LLM Product, and TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel.

steps.action: Open each new node and inspect related-node links
steps.expected: Each node shows required connectivity to existing surfaces
steps.observed: Macro Thesis related links pointed to What is BETTER + Tokenomics; HFT Edge to Architecture + Proof + TRUTH-PERP; LLM Product to Architecture + Tokenomics; TRUTH-PERP to Tokenomics + HFT Edge.

steps.action: Support the UI check with graph data inspection
steps.expected: Source edge data matches the required connectivity contract
steps.observed: graph-nodes.ts related arrays matched the contract for all four new nodes.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed across overview and node-to-node traversal; agent-browser errors checks returned no entries.
evidence.network: No API/XHR behavior was required for graph traversal; agent-browser network log returned no captured requests.
sourceInspection: src/content/graph-nodes.ts:53-58 sets Macro Thesis related nodes to what-is-better and tokenomics.
src/content/graph-nodes.ts:120-145 sets HFT Edge related nodes to architecture/proof/truth-perp-flywheel, LLM Product to architecture/tokenomics, and TRUTH-PERP to tokenomics/hft-edge.

assertions.title: All new surfaces use approved BetterCard top-level shells

steps.action: Inspect the four new surface components in source
steps.expected: Each new content-depth surface uses BetterCard as its top-level shell
steps.observed: Macro Thesis, HFT Edge, LLM Product, and TRUTH-PERP surfaces all import BetterCard and render a top-level <BetterCard> wrapper before nested content.

steps.action: Inspect nested card usage in the same files
steps.expected: Nested metric/sub-cards may use direct shadcn Card primitives
steps.observed: Each surface also imports shadcn Card primitives for nested metric cards, matching the contract that only the top-level shell must be BetterCard.

steps.action: Confirm the real browser surfaces match the expected shell structure
steps.expected: Focused surfaces render inside consistent BetterCard-based shells
steps.observed: All four focused surfaces rendered with the same glass-card shell treatment visible in the browser screenshots.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed while opening each new surface.
evidence.network: No API/XHR behavior was required for this shell-contract check.
sourceInspection: src/components/graph/surfaces/MacroThesisSurface.tsx:4-32 imports BetterCard and uses a top-level <BetterCard>; nested metric cards use shadcn Card at line 60.
src/components/graph/surfaces/HftEdgeSurface.tsx:4-32 imports BetterCard and uses a top-level <BetterCard>; nested cards use shadcn Card at lines 47 and 68.
src/components/graph/surfaces/LlmProductSurface.tsx:4-32 imports BetterCard and uses a top-level <BetterCard>; nested metric cards use shadcn Card at line 47.
src/components/graph/surfaces/TruthPerpFlywheelSurface.tsx:4-32 imports BetterCard and uses a top-level <BetterCard>; nested metric cards use shadcn Card at line 47.

assertions.title: Maturity labels on all new content

steps.action: Open each of the four new surfaces in the graph shell
steps.expected: Every new content block carries visible maturity labels
steps.observed: Each new surface showed visible MaturityBadge labels on the overview shell and on each section/phase block; DOM text checks counted 5 badges on Macro Thesis, 6 on HFT Edge, 6 on LLM Product, and 8 on TRUTH-PERP.

steps.action: Inspect enriched content on existing surfaces
steps.expected: Architecture, Tokenomics, and Proof enrichments also carry maturity labels
steps.observed: Architecture, Tokenomics, and Proof enrichment cards each displayed MaturityBadge chips in the browser.

steps.expected: New and enriched surface components explicitly render MaturityBadge
steps.observed: Source inspection found MaturityBadge usage in all four new surfaces plus ArchitectureSurface, TokenomicsSurface, and ProofSurface.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed while validating maturity labels on new and enriched surfaces.
evidence.network: No API/XHR behavior was required for this label check.
sourceInspection: src/components/graph/surfaces/MacroThesisSurface.tsx:13 and 35 render MaturityBadge for the top-level shell and each claim.
src/components/graph/surfaces/HftEdgeSurface.tsx:13 and 35 render MaturityBadge for the shell and each section.
src/components/graph/surfaces/LlmProductSurface.tsx:13 and 35 render MaturityBadge for the shell and each section.
src/components/graph/surfaces/TruthPerpFlywheelSurface.tsx:13, 35, and 86 render MaturityBadge for the shell, sections, and flywheel phases.
src/components/graph/surfaces/ArchitectureSurface.tsx:37, TokenomicsSurface.tsx:36, and ProofSurface.tsx:25/53 render MaturityBadge on enriched existing-surface blocks.

assertions.title: New nodes reachable via graph navigation

steps.action: Load the default graph workspace at /
steps.expected: New nodes are reachable from the default state without relying on direct hashes
steps.observed: The default graph overview visibly exposed direct buttons for all four new nodes in the primary atlas grid.

steps.action: Use real graph navigation to open each new node
steps.expected: Each node opens within two clicks from the default state
steps.observed: Macro Thesis, HFT Edge, LLM Product, and TRUTH-PERP each opened successfully from the default overview in one click, with the URL updating to the focused #graph-* state.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed during default-state graph navigation.
evidence.network: No API/XHR behavior was required for this traversal flow.
sourceInspection: Default graph controls are rendered by GraphShell as graph-node-button elements and the new nodes are registered in src/content/graph-nodes.ts.

assertions.title: Seamless navigation between old and new surfaces

steps.action: Traverse between existing and new surfaces in the same graph session
steps.expected: Switching between old and new surfaces preserves the same shell language and transition behavior
steps.observed: Navigation from Architecture → Tokenomics → Macro Thesis and back through the graph shell kept the same focused-surface container, typography scale, border treatment, and related-link layout.

steps.action: Compare old/new browser renders and supporting source
steps.expected: New surfaces feel visually consistent with older surfaces
steps.observed: Architecture/Tokenomics/Proof and the new surfaces all rendered inside the same BetterCard-based graph shell and used the same mono labels, white typography, and translucent nested cards.

evidence.consoleErrors: none observed while switching between old and new graph surfaces.
evidence.network: No API/XHR behavior was required for this styling/transition consistency check.
sourceInspection: src/components/ui/BetterCard.tsx defines the shared shell treatment used across graph surfaces.
Existing and new surface components in src/components/graph/surfaces all import the same BetterCard wrapper and shadcn card primitives.

assertions.title: Mobile responsiveness for new surfaces

steps.action: Set the browser viewport to 375x812 and open each new surface
steps.expected: All new surfaces render at 375px width without horizontal overflow
steps.observed: Macro Thesis, HFT Edge, LLM Product, and TRUTH-PERP all rendered without document or surface horizontal overflow (docOverflow=0 and surfaceOverflow=0 for each surface).

steps.action: Measure interactive target sizes on the mobile focused surfaces
steps.expected: Tap targets remain touch-friendly on mobile
steps.observed: The focused-surface back button measured 28x28px and related-node buttons measured 26px tall across the new surfaces (for example 136x26, 122x26, 107x26, 94x26), which is below a touch-friendly ~44px target.

evidence.network: No API/XHR behavior was required for this mobile rendering check.
mobileMeasurements: Macro Thesis: docOverflow=0, surfaceOverflow=0, back button 28x28px, related buttons 136x26px and 107x26px.
HFT Edge: docOverflow=0, surfaceOverflow=0, back button 28x28px, related buttons 122x26px, 129x26px, and 186x26px.
LLM Product: docOverflow=0, surfaceOverflow=0, back button 28x28px, related buttons 122x26px and 107x26px.
TRUTH-PERP & Flywheel: docOverflow=0, surfaceOverflow=0, back button 28x28px, related buttons 107x26px and 94x26px.

assertions.issues: Layout width is responsive, but the focused-surface navigation controls are too small for touch-first use at 375px.

summary: Tested 8 assigned assertions. 7 passed and 1 failed: VAL-CROSS-018 failed because the new surfaces fit within 375px without overflow, but the back/related navigation controls on the mobile focused shell are only 26-28px tall instead of touch-friendly targets.
